# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.119.0/http/server.ts";
import { KV } from "https://deno.land/x/kv@v1.6.2/mod.ts";

const app = serve({ port: 80 });

// Create a new KV store for the API
const usersKv = new KV("users");

app.get("/search", async (request, response) => {
  // Parse the request parameters
  const query = request.url.query;
  const filterParam = query.filterParam || "";

  // Create a key for the userId based on the filterParam
  const userIdKey = `users/${query.userId}/${filterParam}`;

  try {
    // Get the value from the KV store using the key
    const value = await usersKv.get(userIdKey);

    if (value) {
      // If a value was found, return it in the response
      response.send(value);
    } else {
      // If no value was found, return an error message
      response.status = 404;
      response.body = "Not found";
    }
  } catch (error) {
    console.log(error);

    // Return an error message if there was an issue with the KV store
    response.status = 500;
    response.body = "Internal server error";
  }
});
